steps.title: Parse the sentence structure
steps.content: The sentence reads: “John Donne’s ______ much admired during his lifetime … had … been essentially gathering dust.” The blank follows the possessive “Donne’s” and must supply a noun. The phrase “much admired during his lifetime (1572–1631) and in the decades that followed” is a nonrestrictive participial modifier — it needs to be set off by commas on both sides. The main verb is “had … been gathering dust.”

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — works,: supplies the noun and the opening comma needed to bracket the participial phrase — the sentence reads smoothly as “Donne’s works, much admired … and in the decades that followed, had … been gathering dust.” ✓
  • Choice B — works, were: inserts a finite verb “were” inside the modifier, creating a second independent clause that clashes with the main verb “had been.” ✗
  • Choice C — works were: turns the participial phrase into a relative clause without a conjunction, producing a run-on. ✗
  • Choice D — works had been: introduces a competing finite verb phrase that breaks the sentence boundary. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
